meaning: This is not a generic locker set. It is a separate carpentry/prep operation before locker placement.
implied_operations: measure locker run and base depth | frame base or curb | anchor base to wall/floor as applicable | shim and level base | install plywood or runner decking if base type requires | set lockers on base | continue standard locker anchoring/alignment
when_to_add_labor: Always add separate labor before locker installation unless proposal explicitly says base by others.
common_assumptions: Base height and toe-kick detail are shown on drawings. | Locker dimensions are known before base fabrication. | Substrate is suitable for anchorage.
risk_flags: If base detail is missing, add field verify / detail-required flag. | If existing slab is uneven, elevate leveling labor. | If finish carpentry appearance matters, do not bury labor under generic locker minutes.

meaning: Partition style determines anchoring, hardware, alignment, and install sequence.
implied_operations: layout stalls from drawings | locate brackets and anchorage points | verify wall backing at required points | anchor to floor | plumb stiles and panels | set overhead brace | hang doors and adjust hardware for true openings
when_to_add_labor: Whenever partition mounting type is specified; labor differs from ceiling-hung and floor-to-ceiling conditions.
common_assumptions: Backing exists where required | Finished floor allows anchors | Layout dimensions are reliable
risk_flags: Uneven floor or out-of-plumb walls increase install difficulty. | Accessible stall geometry may require more layout verification than stall count suggests.

meaning: Install is code-sensitive and often backing-sensitive; not just quantity times SKU.
implied_operations: verify location and mounting height | confirm backing or reinforcement | choose substrate-appropriate fasteners | install and final-check clearances
when_to_add_labor: Always; elevate further for existing finished restroom, tile/masonry substrate, or unknown backing.
common_assumptions: Blocking/backing exists or is by others unless priced. | Heights and locations come from drawings or field verification.
risk_flags: Unknown backing in existing walls | Tile or CMU drilling | Conflicts with dispenser clearances or grab bar position

meaning: Cabinet type affects projection compliance and wall-condition scope.
implied_operations: confirm cabinet type | check wall condition and recess requirement | review protrusion risk in path of travel | install at approved location
when_to_add_labor: Whenever cabinet type appears; separate cut-in/recess framing from surface mount logic.
common_assumptions: Wall condition matches cabinet type | Location is approved by drawings/code review
risk_flags: Surface-mounted cabinet may violate projection constraints in circulation path. | Recessed units may require framing/cut-in by others unless included.
